title: The Effect of Medicaid Reimbursement Rates on Nursing Home Staffing

analysis.legacy_reason: Requires custom preprocessing (weekly→quarterly aggregation, merge with ccn_high_mcaid_il.csv, bubble-period exclusion, multi-scenario high/low heterogeneity) implemented in results/254/id254_script.R. Template produces only TWFE+CS-NT for main scenario; bundled results.csv retains the full 10-column schema including heterogeneity estimates.

notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED — custom discretized treatment, no standard ES. Gandhi et al. (2024). DISCRETIZED treatment (created by analyst, not paper). Paper uses continuous treatment (illinois * post) for main spec and heterogeneity by Medicaid share (high_mcaid_il / low_mcaid_il) as robustness. We create binary treatment: high-Medicaid IL facilities (342 CCNs, above-median Medicaid share) = treated, low-Medicaid IL facilities (308 CCNs) = control. Single treatment date: Q3 2022 (first post-bubble quarter). Bubble (Jan-Jun 2022) excluded. Weekly data aggregated to quarterly for CS. Custom script: id254_script.R. | AUDIT 2026-04-19: Paper-auditor NOT_APPLICABLE is LEGITIMATE — paper's headline is IL-vs-non-IL pooled DD (β≈5.35 in Figure 2a). Our spec is within-IL high-vs-low Medicaid share DiD (β=5.49), a different estimand entirely. Numerical closeness (2.6%) is coincidence. Heterogeneity confirmed: high=8.84, low=1.72 (both positive). CS-NT=5.69 validates TWFE direction/magnitude. 0 controls → no Spec A/B/C. LOW rating reflects design mismatch documentation, not implementation bug. Paper doesn't report comparable static TWFE at our design level.
